title: AI Agents Authentication: How Autonomous Systems Prove Identity
snippet: AI agents need to authenticate with numerous systems, making AI authentication a crucial security boundary that determines blast radius, revocability, and long-term governance risk.

title: OpenAI's Coding Agent Flaw Exposed GitHub Passwords
snippet: A flaw in OpenAI's coding agent exposed GitHub login credentials of developers, allowing attackers to access private code repositories without authorization via stolen authentication keys.

title: Hack the AI agent: Build agentic AI security skills with the GitHub Secure Code Game
snippet: GitHub's Secure Code Game challenges developers to find and fix security vulnerabilities in agentic AI workflows, including authentication and authorization flaws.

title: Weekly Update – April 20, 2026 | GitHub Agentic Workflows
snippet: GitHub introduced pre-agent-steps functionality to run custom authentication setup before AI agents start, addressing agent identity and credential management issues.

title: GitHub's AI Agent Crisis Forces Microsoft to Tap AWS as Outages Break Enterprise SLAs
snippet: GitHub faced significant platform stability issues related to AI agent traffic, with availability problems caused by rapid load growth and inability to shed load from misbehaving agent clients.

title: Agent Identity (AID) - Authentication & Authorization for AI Agents | Open Source
snippet: Open protocol for AI agent authentication and authorization. Agents prove identity with Ed25519 signatures and receive scoped JWT tokens via OAuth 2.0. MIT licensed.

title: Show HN: Agent Passport – OAuth-like identity verification for AI agents
snippet: Open-source identity verification layer for AI agents providing OAuth-like authentication and authorization flows.

title: Introducing the Agent Governance Toolkit: Open-source runtime security for AI agents | Microsoft
snippet: Includes Agent Mesh with cryptographic identity using DIDs (Ed25519) and Inter-Agent Trust Protocol (IATP) for secure agent-to-agent communication.

title: Introducing HUMAN Verified AI Agent: An Open-Source Foundation for Trustworthy Agent Identity
snippet: Open-source framework for cryptographically authenticated agent communication using HTTP Message Signatures (RFC 9421). Agents sign every request; gateway verifies via public key registry.

title: Verified Agent Identity – BillionsNetwork
snippet: Human-agent linking via signed challenges over DIDs, with proof generation and verification capabilities for agent identity.

title: Set up Agent Auth (Beta) - Docs by LangChain
snippet: LangChain's official Agent Auth documentation covering the authenticate() API, OAuth 2.0 flows (Authorization Code, On-Behalf-Of, Client Credentials), delegated vs direct access modes for agent authentication.

title: Agent Authentication & Authorization — LangChain Agent Security Explained
snippet: Overview of agent authentication (verifying identity) and authorization (controlling access) in LangChain, covering OAuth flows, delegated access, and direct access patterns for securing AI agents.

title: Securing LangChain's MCP Integration: Agent-Based Security for Enterprise AI
snippet: Discussion of securing LangChain's MCP integration with agent-based security patterns for enterprise AI deployments, including identity protocols and cryptographic audit trails.

title: How to Secure LangChain Agents with Cryptographic Signatures — Tutorial
snippet: Tutorial on the Agent Identity Protocol (AIP), an open-source tool giving agents a local crypto wallet to sign actions and create non-repudiable audit trails for LangChain agent security.

title: Use LangChain with Vertex AI Agent Engine
snippet: Google Cloud documentation on using LangChain agents with Vertex AI Agent Engine, including authentication setup and configuration for agent-based workflows.

title: Agent-to-Agent (A2A) Protocol - CrewAI
snippet: CrewAI treats A2A as a first-class delegation primitive, enabling agents to delegate tasks, request information, and collaborate with remote agents. In client mode, agents autonomously choose between local execution and remote delegation based on task requirements.

title: feat: implement hierarchical agent delegation with allowed_agents parameter
snippet: Implements controlled hierarchical agent delegation via allowed_agents parameter. Supports both role strings and agent instances, case-insensitive role matching, and backward compatibility where None means allow all agents.

title: [FEATURE] Allow delegation to specific agents only
snippet: Currently setting allow_delegation=True lets the agent delegate to all agents in the crew. Feature request to restrict delegation to specific agents via target_agents=[] alongside allow_delegation=True.

title: [Feature] Trust-verified task delegation with Agent Governance Toolkit
snippet: When CrewAI agents delegate tasks or invoke tools, there is no built-in mechanism to enforce organizational policies — e.g., which agents can delegate to whom, what data can flow between tasks, or whether human approval is required. Proposes governance callbacks and middleware.

title: Hierarchical AI Agents: A Guide to CrewAI Delegation
snippet: Guide to CrewAI's hierarchical delegation model covering role-based agent orchestration, manager agents, task routing, and structured multi-agent workflows with authorization controls.

title: Agent Identity and Lifecycle — AutoGen
snippet: Agent ID uniquely identifies an agent instance within an agent runtime, including distributed runtime, and serves as the address for receiving messages. Agent types are defined by application code while agent keys are generated from messages.

title: Agentic Identity and Access Management (AIAM) — microsoft/autogen Discussion #5921
snippet: AIAM aims to provide each agent with a unique verifiable identity, enforce fine-grained access permissions, and ensure all actions are auditable within AutoGen multi-agent systems.

title: Zero-Trust Agents: Adding Identity and Access to Multi-Agent Workflows
snippet: Integrates identity and access management into enterprise agentic workflows using a zero-trust approach where each AI agent has identity and issued access tokens for every action, ensuring no implicit trust between entities.

title: [Feature] Governance extension for AutoGen — policy enforcement and agent identity · Issue #7613
snippet: Proposes policy enforcement on tool calls, agent identity verification through IATP-based handshakes, and action audit logging as a governance extension for AutoGen.

title: AutoGen Implementation Patterns: Building Production-Ready Multi-Agent AI Systems
snippet: Covers implementation patterns for building production-ready multi-agent AI systems with AutoGen, including agent identity management, lifecycle patterns, and secure communication between agents.

title: Visa and Partners Complete Secure AI Transactions, Setting the Stage for Mainstream Adoption in 2026
snippet: Visa and 10+ partners introduced the Trusted Agent Protocol, an open framework for safe agent-driven checkout. Hundreds of controlled real-world agent-initiated transactions completed in production environments.

title: User Wallets (Programmable Key Pairs) | Lit Protocol
snippet: PKPs are decentralized key pairs generated via MPC + TSS and TEEs. Private key shares are distributed across Lit nodes in sealed encrypted VMs — the complete key never exists in its entirety. Represented as ERC-721 NFTs.
